examples.title: LSR Forwarding Process
examples.description: Pseudocode showing how an LSR processes an incoming labeled packet
examples.code:
FUNCTION ProcessIncomingPacket(packet)
 BEGIN
 // Extract incoming label
 incomingLabel <- packet.extractLabel()
 
 // Lookup in LFIB
 LFIBentry <- LFIB.lookup(incomingLabel)
 
 IF LFIBentry != NULL THEN
 // Get outgoing operation
 operation <- LFIBentry.operation
 outgoingLabel <- LFIBentry.outgoingLabel
 outgoingInterface <- LFIBentry.interface
 
 SWITCH operation OF
 CASE PUSH:
 packet.pushLabel(outgoingLabel)
 CASE SWAP:
 packet.swapLabel(outgoingLabel)
 CASE POP:
 packet.popLabel()
 END SWITCH
 
 // Forward packet
 send(packet, outgoingInterface)
 ELSE
 // Drop packet (no valid label mapping)
 drop(packet)
 END IF
 END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the basic forwarding logic in an LSR. It extracts the incoming label, looks up the LFIB for the corresponding action, performs the appropriate label operation (PUSH, SWAP, or POP), and forwards the packet out the correct interface.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) - Exact match lookup in LFIB
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) - Where n is number of LFIB entries

examples.title: LDP Label Distribution Process
examples.description: Pseudocode showing how LDP establishes LSP by exchanging label mappings
examples.code:
PROCEDURE LDP_Session_Establishment(neighborRouter)
 BEGIN
 // Send LDP Discovery Hello
 sendHello(neighborRouter)
 
 // Receive Hello and establish adjacency
 IF receiveHello(neighborRouter) THEN
 session <- createSession(neighborRouter)
 sendKeepalive(session)
 END IF
 END

PROCEDURE LDP_Mapping_Exchange(FEC)
 BEGIN
 // Request label for FEC
 sendLabelRequest(localFEC, nextHop)
 
 // Receive label mapping from neighbor
 labelMapping <- receiveLabelMapping()
 
 // Store in LIB
 LIB.addBinding(FEC, labelMapping)
 
 // Select best label and install in LFIB
 bestLabel <- LIB.selectBestLabel(FEC)
 LFIB.install(FEC, bestLabel)
 END
examples.explanation: This code shows the LDP process for establishing LSPs. First, LSRs exchange Hello messages to discover neighbors and establish sessions. Then they exchange label mappings for FECs, storing bindings in LIB and installing the best available labels in LFIB for forwarding.
examples.timeComplexity: O(k) - Where k is number of FECs
examples.spaceComplexity: O(m) - Where m is LIB entries

examples.title: FEC Classification at Ingress LSR
examples.description: Pseudocode showing how ingress LSR classifies packets into FECs
examples.code:
FUNCTION ClassifyPacketIntoFEC(packet)
 BEGIN
 // Extract packet header information
 destIP <- packet.getDestinationIP()
 sourceIP <- packet.getSourceIP()
 tos <- packet.getTypeOfService()
 
 // Check FEC classification rules
 FOR EACH fecRule IN FEC_Rules_Table DO
 IF destIP matches fecRule.prefix AND
 tos matches fecRule.qos_requirement THEN
 RETURN fecRule.fecID
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Default FEC if no match
 RETURN DEFAULT_FEC
 END

FUNCTION IngressLSR_Process(packet)
 BEGIN
 // Classify packet
 fecID <- ClassifyPacketIntoFEC(packet)
 
 // Lookup label for this FEC
 outLabel <- LFIB.getLabel(fecID)
 
 // Push label onto packet
 packet.pushLabel(outLabel)
 
 // Forward to next hop
 outgoingInterface <- LFIB.getInterface(fecID)
 send(packet, outgoingInterface)
 END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the classification process at the ingress LSR. It examines packet headers to determine which FEC the packet belongs to, then looks up the corresponding label and performs label imposition before forwarding.
examples.timeComplexity: O(m) - Where m is number of FEC rules

examples.title: Penultimate Hop Popping Logic
examples.description: Pseudocode showing PHP optimization at penultimate LSR
examples.code:
FUNCTION PenultimateHop_Process(packet)
 BEGIN
 incomingLabel <- packet.getOuterLabel()
 
 // Lookup in LFIB
 lfibEntry <- LFIB.lookup(incomingLabel)
 
 // Check if next hop is egress and PHP is enabled
 IF lfibEntry.outgoingLabel == IMPLICIT_NULL AND
 lfibEntry.nextHopIsEgress == TRUE THEN
 // Perform Penultimate Hop Popping
 packet.popLabel()
 
 // Forward unlabeled packet to egress
 send(packet, lfibEntry.interface)
 ELSE
 // Normal label swap
 packet.swapLabel(lfibEntry.outgoingLabel)
 send(packet, lfibEntry.interface)
 END IF
 END
examples.explanation: This code shows how PHP works. The penultimate LSR checks if the outgoing label is Implicit Null (label 3) and if the next hop is the egress router. If both conditions are true, it removes (pops) the label instead of swapping, sending an unlabeled IP packet to the egress router.
